text:
Absolutely the worst banking experience I have ever had.

My account was suddenly frozen, and I was asked to submit multiple documents and transaction proofs. I provided everything they requested immediately, yet days passed without any meaningful response or resolution. Every follow-up resulted in the same generic replies like “under review” or “please wait.”

Keeping a customer’s money inaccessible without timely updates is completely unacceptable. The customer support is extremely slow, unhelpful, and shows no urgency in resolving genuine issues.

A bank is built on trust, but this experience has completely destroyed my confidence in both Jupiter and Federal Bank. If something goes wrong with your account, don’t expect prompt communication or efficient support.

I cannot recommend this service to anyone based on my experience. There are far better banking options available that value their customers and provide proper support when it actually matters.
title: Worst banking experience ever

text:
This review is based on 5 years of banking experience with Jupiter.

I’ve used multiple traditional banks as well as fintech apps like Fi and Niyo, and Jupiter has been the most consistent experience by far. The app is fast, reliable, and I’ve never faced delayed transaction updates or unnecessary hassles.

The rewards program has provided real value—I’ve redeemed rewards into digital gold and earned benefits worth around ₹6000 over the years. The salary account perks and low charges make the overall experience feel premium without the premium cost.

Jupiter remains my top choice. Highly recommended.
title: 5 Years Later, Still My Favorite Banking App

text:
I would strongly advise against using Jupiter for online banking. My account was frozen and placed under lien, and despite multiple attempts to get help, I received no meaningful support or resolution.

1-What was most frustrating was the lack of human assistance. Not once was I connected with someone who could properly address my issue. The support team kept providing generic responses without offering any practical solution.

2- If this had happened with a traditional bank, I could have visited a branch and spoken to someone directly. With Jupiter, there was no such option, leaving me stuck without access to my own money and with no clear path to resolution.

3- Banking is built on trust and support during difficult situations. Unfortunately, my experience with Jupiter was the opposite. If you ever face an account issue, be prepared for a potentially long and stressful experience.

4- I am sharing this review so others can make an informed decision before choosing this platform. If anyone would like to know more about my experience, feel free to reach out and I can explain exactly what happened with my account.
title: Strongly not Recommended

text:
I’m giving this app 1 star — and that’s being generous.

The biggest issue is the complete lack of real customer support. When something goes wrong (and it will), you’re left dealing with automated responses that don’t actually solve anything. There’s no reliable way to get timely, human help.

On top of that, the charges are not transparent. There are hidden fees and surprise deductions that just show up in your statement without clear explanation. This completely breaks trust, especially for a financial app where clarity is the bare minimum expectation.

Even worse, if you decide to close your account, they still charge you for it. Charging users to exit after a poor experience is unacceptable.

Overall, this app feels misleading and poorly managed. Would not recommend to anyone who values transparency and basic customer support.
title: I’m giving this app 1 star — and that’s being generous

text:
I have been an HDFC Bank customer for years, but I am extremely disappointed by the decision to block access to the banking app unless users upgrade to iOS 17.

I use an iPhone X, which Apple itself does not support beyond iOS 16. This means HDFC is effectively forcing customers to purchase a new phone just to access their bank account. That is neither reasonable nor customer-friendly.

I fully understand the importance of security and keeping software updated. However, many banks around the world continue to support older operating systems while implementing appropriate risk controls, warnings, restricted features, or alternative authentication methods. Completely locking out customers who own perfectly functional devices is an excessive approach.

The relationship here is between the customer and the bank…not between the customer and Apple. Customers should not be forced into expensive hardware upgrades simply to perform basic banking activities.

I urge HDFC Bank to reconsider this policy and provide a supported version for older devices or at least a limited-access mode for customers who understand and accept the associated risks. Banking services should remain accessible, inclusive, and customer-centric.

Please review this decision and provide a practical solution for customers using older iPhones or be ready for customers to start closing their banking relation with you.
title: Unfair policy, Forced ios17 Update & No Service

text:
Very disappointed with HDFC Bank after 7 years of banking.

Recently, HDFC closed my Zumbo loan without informing me. No call, no SMS, no email. The EMI was deducted normally in April, but just one day before the May EMI due date, they suddenly sent an email asking me to pay the full outstanding amount because the loan was already closed. Due to the lack of notice, the auto-payment bounced, and they put my account on hold.

After countless calls and emails, there is still no resolution. They promised to remove the charges if I paid the closure amount, but after payment, they generated a bill for the same charges. Every time I call, they say, "Please wait 2 days, we have raised a request." It's been more than a month, and nothing has been resolved.

Customer support is terrible. Every call connects to a different person, and I have to explain everything again and again. Nobody takes ownership of the issue.

They charge unnecessary fees without proper intimation. In my case, even after informing them about an issue with my account, they charged a ₹500 credit card renewal fee and then another ₹450 renewal/return-related charge within 24 hours.

Another joke is their branch system. HDFC has so many branches, but every nearby branch asks customers to visit their "main branch" even for small issues. Then what's the purpose of having so many branches?

I also raised a complaint regarding my debit card. Instead of helping me, an employee called and shouted at me for raising a complaint because it would affect their incentives and performance points. They seem more worried about employee ratings than customer problems.

I have all the call recordings and emails. I never expected such poor service, poor communication, and poor customer handling from HDFC Bank. I would not recommend HDFC accounts, loans, or credit cards to anyone.
title: Worst bank experience with HDFC
